info.title: trouve harness protocol
info.description: Commands are POST endpoints; state flows back on cursor-addressed SSE event streams.

get.summary: Raw bytes of a stored prompt attachment, with its uploaded MIME type.
Static catalog of pre-canned automations for common development tasks.

post.summary:
Fire the automation immediately (in the background); the outcome shows
up on the automation's last_* fields and an `automation.fired` event.

delete.summary:
Remove the managed install of a CLI (a system install found on PATH is
untouched and will be used again if present).
Cancel an in-flight install; the CLI returns to its previous state.

post.summary: Register a self-hosted GitHub Enterprise instance.

delete.summary: Remove an enterprise host (and forget its stored secrets).

put.summary:
Enable or disable local models. Disabling stops the llama-server
sidecar and unregisters the "local" provider.

delete.summary: Cancel an in-flight model download; the partial file is deleted.

get.summary:
Search HuggingFace for GGUF repos, with per-file hardware-fit guidance
for this machine.

post.summary:
Restart llama-server with the model it is serving (reload happens in
the background; poll `GET /v1/local` for server_status).

get.summary:
PTY output as SSE: each event's `id` is the byte offset *after* the
chunk, data is base64. `?after=` resumes from an offset (bytes older
than the retained backlog are silently skipped). A final `exit` event
marks shell exit. Ephemeral — not part of the persisted event log.

200.description: SSE stream of base64 output chunks

post.summary:
Kick an idle thread into draining its queue. Queued prompts never
auto-run at startup (a crash may have cut the previous turn short) and a
failed turn pauses its queue — both wait for this explicit resume.

AddGithubHostRequest.description:
Register a self-hosted GitHub Enterprise instance
(`POST /v1/integrations/github/hosts`).

client_id.description: Client id of an OAuth app on that instance (device flow enabled).

AddLocalModelRequest.description: Add a custom GGUF from a HuggingFace repo to the local model list.

AgentMode.description:
A data-driven agent mode: prompt + tool policy + model/permission defaults.
Adding a mode is configuration, not code (AGENTS.md invariant 6).

allowed_tools.description: Tool names this mode may use; empty means all registered tools.

default_model.description:
Preferred model for threads started in this mode ("provider/model").
None falls back to the global default model.

oneOf.description:
Permission mode for threads started in this mode. None falls back to
the global default permission mode.

default_thinking_level.description:
Preferred thinking level for threads started in this mode. The value
is a model-advertised enum token (for example "medium" or "high").
None falls back to the global default thinking level.

read_only.description:
When true the mode can never mutate the worktree regardless of the
thread's permission mode (e.g. plan/question modes).

system_prompt.description: Appended to the base system prompt.

ApprovalDecision.description: Permission decision for an approval request.

Attachment.description:
A stored prompt attachment. Bytes are served at
`GET /v1/attachments/{id}`.

AttachmentUpload.description: One file uploaded with a prompt.

mime.description:
MIME type ("image/png"). `image/*` attachments are passed to agents
as native image inputs; anything else is referenced by path.

name.description:
Display name ("screenshot.png"); the server keeps it for rendering
and derives the stored file's extension from it.

Automation.description:
A scheduled prompt. Each run creates a fresh session (worktree) in the
workspace, a thread with the configured mode/model, and sends the
prompt — exactly as if the user had typed it.

last_error.description: Why the last run failed ("" = it didn't).

last_session_id.description: Session created by the last run.

mode.description: Agent mode for the runs (None = the default mode).

model.description: Model for the runs (None = the mode's default).

permission_mode.description:
Permission policy applied only to sessions created by this automation.
Defaults to Ask; Yolo is an explicit unattended-execution opt-in.

AutomationSchedule.description: When an automation fires. Times are the server's local time zone.

days.description: Weekly: days it fires (0 = Monday … 6 = Sunday); at least one.

AutomationTemplate.description:
A pre-canned automation for a common development task
(`GET /v1/automations/templates`). Clients use these to pre-fill the
create form; the user still picks the workspace and can edit anything.

description.description: One-line summary shown in template pickers.

BranchList.description: Local branches of a workspace repository, for base-ref selection.

head.description: The branch HEAD currently points at (default selection).

CliInfo.description:
A vendor CLI trouve can download and manage (cursor-agent, claude,
codex), with its current install state.

id.description: Stable id, also the binary name: "cursor-agent", "claude", "codex".

installed_version.description: Version of the binary trouve would run, when one was resolved.

kinds.description: Provider kinds served by this CLI (e.g. ["cursor-cli"]).

latest_version.description: Newest version the vendor serves (None when the check failed).

path.description: Absolute path of the resolved binary, when known.

source.description:
Where that binary comes from: "managed" (trouve-installed),
"path" (system install), or "none".

CliInstallStatus.description: State of a CLI install started with `POST /v1/clis/{id}/install`.

total_bytes.description: Expected total from Content-Length; 0 when unknown.

version.description: Version being (or just) installed, when known.

CodeReviewJob.description: A durable execution of one model review against one immutable PR head.

reviewer_ids.description:
Reviewer profiles are snapshotted internally; their stable ids are
exposed here for history and diagnostics.

CodeReviewMode.description: Whether an installed repository participates in automated code review.

CodeReviewRepository.description:
One repository visible to a GitHub App installation plus trouve's local
review policy for it.

model.description: Provider-qualified model; absent means the review mode/global default.

reviewer_ids.description: Ordered reviewer profiles run for each revision.

reviewer_overrides.description:
Per-reviewer repository overrides. Entries may be retained while a
reviewer is disabled so re-enabling it restores its configuration.

oneOf.description: Select a newly created thread.
Reveal and attach the current session's integrated terminal.

CommandAction.description:
Client-side action that follows a completed command. Persistent state is
still reported through the event log; these are navigation hints only.

CommandInfo.description:
One slash command or skill accepted by Trouve, surfaced by clients as a
prompt-box completion.

kind.description:
Whether submitting this command starts a model turn or executes a
deterministic Trouve action.

usage.description: User-facing invocation syntax, including the leading slash.

CommandKind.description: How a slash command is dispatched by clients.

CommandResult.description: Result of a deterministic slash command.

action.description: Optional client navigation requested by the command.

ConfigureGithubAppRequest.description:
Configure the GitHub App used for code reviews. The server validates the
private key against GitHub before replacing any stored credentials.

private_key_pem.description: PEM-encoded RSA private key downloaded from the GitHub App settings.

webhook_secret.description:
Secret used to verify `X-Hub-Signature-256`. Empty disables webhooks
and leaves reconciliation polling as the only trigger source.

base.description: Base branch (default: the session's base ref without `origin/`).

base_ref.description: Base ref the session branch is created from (default: workspace HEAD).

checkout_ref.description:
Optional ref used to create the session branch while `base_ref`
remains the comparison base. Automated PR reviews use this to check
out the exact head SHA and still expose the base-to-head diff.

fetch_latest.description:
Fetch the base branch's configured upstream and start from its latest
remote commit. Refs without an upstream are used as-is.

title.description: Human-readable title; also used to derive the branch slug.

model_options.description: Model-specific options validated against the model's options schema.

ErrorBody.description: Uniform error body for non-2xx responses.

oneOf.description:
The turn was interrupted by the user (via the cancel endpoint). Like
`turn.failed` it pauses the queue, but it isn't an error condition.

attachments.description:
Files the user attached to the prompt (bytes at
`GET /v1/attachments/{id}`).

oneOf.description:
Streamed model output. Replaying all deltas of a turn reproduces the
final message exactly.

Streamed model reasoning ("thinking") text, where the provider
exposes it. Display-only: never part of the provider transcript.
Folded final assistant text for the turn.

The agent asked the user one or more questions; the turn is blocked
until `question.resolved`. Clients render an answer wizard.
Answers submitted (or `answers: null` when the user skipped).

Trouve's authoritative slash-command and skill catalog for this
thread. Replaces any previously announced catalog.

A deterministic Trouve command completed. The output is persisted so
replay and every client render the same command history.

Legacy vendor-reported slash commands / skills. Kept for replay of
existing event logs; new turns publish CommandCatalogUpdated instead.

The thread's queue of pending prompts changed (enqueue, edit,
reorder, delete, or dispatch). Carries the full remaining queue in
run order; clients replace any previous list.

The thread's current todo snapshot changed. Historical `todo_write`
tool calls remain in the stream; clients replace this snapshot.

The thread's transcript neared the model's context window; the engine
is summarizing older messages. Clients show a busy indicator.

messages_compacted.description: Provider-transcript messages folded into the summary.

commit.description: Git commit hash the checkpoint points at.

oneOf.description:
An account-centric PR-dashboard refresh completed for one GitHub
instance. Clients replace the previously folded host slice.
Session metadata changed (rename / archive). Clients refetch.
Thread settings changed (mode/model). Clients refetch.

A session started or stopped actively processing prompts (one of its
threads began running turns, or the last active one went idle).
Drives the activity indicator in session lists; `Session.active`
carries the same state for initial fetches.

A scheduled automation ran (or failed to). Clients refetch the
automations list — and the sessions list when it succeeded, since a
run creates a session.
Session the run created (absent when the run failed).

GitHub App configuration, repository policy, or a durable review job
changed. Clients refetch `/v1/code-review` and fold the replacement.

The server's internet reachability changed (it is the one talking to
model vendors, so it owns this state). While offline, `/v1/models`
lists only models that can run without internet (local provider,
loopback endpoints); clients gate prompt entry on having usable
models and announce recovery. `ServerInfo.online` carries the same
state for initial fetches.

Event.description:
Every event type in the log. Serialized with a `type` tag using
dot-namespaced names, per the event-log design doc.

cursor.description:
Strictly increasing within a scope; used as the SSE event id for
`Last-Event-ID` resumption. Not necessarily dense.

ts.description: RFC 3339 timestamp assigned at append time.

EventEnvelope.description: The envelope every event is delivered in (and persisted as).

ExecuteCommandRequest.description:
Execute one deterministic Trouve slash command. Prompt commands from the
catalog continue to use `SendMessageRequest` so they start a model turn.

arguments.description: Everything following the command name, without leading whitespace.

name.description: Catalog name without the leading slash.

GithubAppStatus.description:
Public GitHub App state. Private keys and webhook secrets are never
returned by the protocol.

GithubHostIntegration.description:
Auth state of one GitHub host (github.com or a GitHub Enterprise
instance).

oauth_available.description: A device-flow OAuth app client id is configured for this host.

removable.description: Enterprise hosts can be removed; github.com cannot.

GithubIntegration.description: Whether the GitHub OAuth integration is authenticated.

configured.description: github.com's state (mirrors `hosts[0]`; kept for older clients).

hosts.description:
Every known host: github.com first, then the configured GitHub
Enterprise hosts in config order.

oauth_available.description:
Whether "Sign in with GitHub" (OAuth device flow) is available —
i.e. a GitHub OAuth app client id is configured or built in.

GithubPrList.description:
Pull requests relevant to the authenticated account on one GitHub host,
spanning every repository visible to that account. Includes open PRs the
account authored, was asked to review, or participated in, plus recently
merged relevant PRs.

host.description: GitHub instance this slice came from.

viewer.description:
Login of the authenticated GitHub user ("" when unknown) — clients
use it to spot PRs where that user's review was requested.

KnownProvider.description:
A well-known provider preset: clients offer these for one-click setup
instead of hand-typed base URLs.

api_key_env.description:
Conventional environment variable holding the API key, when one
exists (empty for keyless local providers like Ollama).

auth.description:
How the provider authenticates: "api-key", "oauth" (subscription
login), "cli" (the vendor's own CLI holds subscription auth), or
"none" (keyless local endpoints).

experimental.description:
Uses an undocumented vendor endpoint that may break or be restricted
at any time; clients should display a warning.

LocalGpu.description: A GPU the local-models hardware probe found.

vram_bytes.description: Dedicated VRAM in bytes (system RAM for unified-memory machines).

LocalModelInfo.description:
One local model (curated catalog entry or user-added GGUF) with its
download and hardware-fit state.

context_window.description: Context window trouve serves the model with.

downloaded.description: True when the GGUF is on disk and ready to run.

fit.description:
Hardware fit: "gpu" (fits in VRAM), "cpu" (fits in RAM, slower),
or "too-large".

repo.description: HuggingFace repo the GGUF comes from (e.g. "Qwen/…-GGUF").

LocalSearchFile.description:
One single-file GGUF inside a search result's repo
(`GET /v1/local/search`).

added.description: Already in the local model list (catalog or previously added).

file.description: Path inside the repo, ready for [`AddLocalModelRequest::file`].

fit.description: Hardware fit on this machine: "gpu", "cpu", or "too-large".

quant.description: Quantization tag parsed from the filename ("Q4_K_M"; may be empty).

LocalSearchResult.description:
One HuggingFace repo matching a local-model search, with its
single-file GGUFs (smallest first) and a recommended pick for this
machine's hardware.

recommended.description: Index into `files` of the best pick for this hardware.

LocalStatus.description:
Local inference status: hardware, the llama.cpp runtime install, the
running server, and every known model.

enabled.description:
Whether local models are enabled (the "local" provider is
registered). Toggled with `PUT /v1/local/enabled`.

running_model.description: Model id currently loaded in (or loading into) llama-server.

runtime_installed.description: Whether the llama.cpp runtime (llama-server) is installed.

runtime_latest_version.description:
Newest llama.cpp build the vendor serves (None when the check
failed, e.g. offline).

runtime_managed.description:
True when the runtime is a trouve-managed install (updatable and
uninstallable through the API), false for PATH/system builds.

runtime_update_available.description: True when a managed install is older than `runtime_latest_version`.

LoginStarted.description: Response to starting an OAuth login for a provider.

user_code.description:
Code the user must enter at the verification URL (device flow only;
PKCE flows encode everything in the URL).

verification_url.description: URL the user must open in a browser to approve access.

LoginStatus.description: Current state of a provider's OAuth login attempt.

McpLogs.description: Recent stderr and lifecycle lines for one MCP server.

McpServerInfo.description:
One user-managed MCP server (from `mcp.json` in the trouve config dir or
`.agents/.mcp.json` in a workspace). First-party servers trouve injects
itself (the Claude approval bridge) never appear here.

detail.description: "5 tools" when healthy, the failure reason when not, "" for unknown.

env.description: Values may be `${VAR}` references resolved at spawn time.

health.description:
"ok" / "error" / "unknown" (unknown when listing skipped the probe) /
"untrusted" (a repo-scoped server that is never auto-run).

workspace_id.description:
For workspace scope: which workspace's config this entry lives in.
Empty for user scope.

ModeInfo.description: A mode plus where it came from, for the settings UI.

origin.description:
"builtin" (untouched), "customized" (builtin with a user override
file), "custom" (user-added), or "workspace" (defined in the
workspace's .agents/modes — file-managed, read-only in settings).

ModelInfo.description:
A model a configured provider can run, with enough metadata for clients
to render selection and options UIs generically.

input_price_per_mtok.description: USD per million input tokens (None = unknown; cost reporting skips it).

options_schema.description:
JSON Schema for the model's options object (thinking level, etc.).
Clients render these controls from the schema, not from hardcoded
per-model knowledge.

OpenTerminalRequest.description: Open (or re-attach to) the session's terminal.

cols.description: Initial grid size; ignored when re-attaching to a live terminal.

PermissionMode.description: How tool calls are gated in a thread. See ADR 0004.

head_sha.description: Exact pull-request head commit. Older servers omitted this field.

host.description: GitHub instance that owns this PR.

last_comment_at.description: When the newest comment (of either kind) was posted.

mergeable.description:
Whether GitHub can merge this PR cleanly (false = merge/rebase
conflicts). None while unknown: list endpoints omit it and GitHub
computes it lazily even on single-PR reads.

requested_reviewers.description: Logins with an outstanding review request.

workspace_id.description: Matching local workspace, when one is registered.

ProviderCapabilityMode.description: Who controls the agent-facing capability surface for this provider.

ProviderInfo.description: A configured provider, with secrets elided.

auth.description:
"api-key", "oauth", "cli" (vendor CLI holds the subscription auth),
or "none" — drives which credential UI to show.

capability_mode.description:
Whether the provider receives Trouve's authoritative agent surface or
runs with a mixed vendor/Trouve compatibility surface.

category.description:
Presentation/billing category: "subscription", "api", or "local".
This is independent of `auth`: a subscription such as Kimi Code can
still authenticate with an API key.

experimental.description:
Uses an undocumented vendor endpoint that may break or be restricted
at any time; clients should display a warning.

has_credentials.description:
Whether usable credentials were resolved (key, env, OAuth, or a
logged-in vendor CLI).

default_model.description: Default model for new threads, e.g. "openai/gpt-4.1-mini".

default_permission_mode.description:
Global default permission mode for new threads, used by modes without
a default of their own. Absent on older servers means Ask.

default_thinking_level.description:
Global thinking level for new threads. None leaves the selected
model at its own default.

Question.description:
A single question inside a `question.requested` event. Clients always
offer a trailing free-form "Other" choice in addition to the listed
options; its text comes back in [`QuestionAnswer::other_text`].

allow_multiple.description: Multiple options may be selected (checkboxes instead of radios).

other_text.description: Free-form text when the user picked "Other".

selected_option_ids.description: Ids of the selected options (at most one unless `allow_multiple`).

QueuedPrompt.description:
A prompt waiting its turn. Queued prompts persist on disk and run in
`position` order once the thread is idle.

attachments.description: Attachments uploaded with the prompt (already stored server-side).

path.description: Absolute path to a git repository root.

ReorderQueueRequest.description:
Full desired order for a thread's queue (every queued prompt id, first
to run first).

ResolveQuestionRequest.description:
Answers for a pending `question.requested`. `answers: null` skips the
questions (the agent is told the user declined to answer).

ReviewerOverride.description: Repository-specific changes layered over a reusable reviewer profile.

model.description:
Provider-qualified model. Absent means inherit the profile, which in
turn may inherit the repository/default model.

ReviewerProfile.description:
Configuration for one focused reviewer. Built-in profiles are shipped by
trouve; custom profiles are user-managed and may choose their own model.

Scope.description: Which stream an event belongs to. Cursors are monotonic per scope.

attachments.description:
Files riding along with the prompt (screenshots, logs, …); bytes are
base64 in the request, stored server-side, and referenced by id from
then on.

online.description:
Whether the server can currently reach the internet (see the
`server.connectivity_changed` event). Absent on older servers, which
never report offline.

active.description:
One of the session's threads is actively processing prompts right
now. Live updates ride the server-scope `session.activity` event.

archived.description:
Archived sessions are hidden from default listings but keep their
worktree and history.

worktree_path.description: Absolute path of the session worktree.

SessionDiff.description: The session's unified diff against its base ref.

default_thinking_level.description:
Global thinking level for the selected model. Omitted when the model
has no thinking knob, preserving the existing global setting for
models that do support it.

SetDefaultPermissionModeRequest.description:
Set the global default permission mode
(`PUT /v1/config/default-permission-mode`).

SetLocalEnabledRequest.description:
Turn local models on or off (`PUT /v1/local/enabled`). Disabling stops
the llama-server sidecar and unregisters the "local" provider.

SetSkillsSettingsRequest.description: Update the global skill settings persisted in `config.toml`.

SkillsSettings.description:
Global skill settings. Built-in skills are enabled by default; user and
workspace skills are independent of this switch.

SubscriptionHealth.description: Subscription usage for one configured provider.

note.description: Human explanation for unavailable/unsupported; "" when ok.

plan.description: Plan name as reported ("plus", "pro", …); "" when unknown.

status.description:
"ok" (windows below), "unavailable" (vendor query failed / not
logged in), or "unsupported" (vendor doesn't share the data).

SubscriptionWindow.description:
One metered rate-limit window of a vendor subscription (e.g. Codex's
5-hour and weekly buckets).

resets.description: Pre-rendered reset note ("resets in 2h 10m"), "" when unknown.

exited.description: True once the shell process has exited (the stream is complete).

TerminalInputRequest.description: Keyboard/paste bytes for the PTY, base64-encoded.

model_options.description:
Current values for the model's options (thinking level, etc.);
clients render controls from the model's `options_schema`.

spawned.description:
True when an agent spawned this thread (spawn_thread/spawn_session
tools) rather than the user; clients mark such threads visually.

todos.description:
Current todo snapshot for this thread. Tool-call events retain the
history of how the list changed; this field is the initial-load view.

TodoItem.description: One stable item in a thread's current todo list.

TodoStatus.description: Progress state for one item in a thread's current todo list.

ToolStatus.description: Terminal status of a tool call.

TurnAccepted.description:
Accepted-for-processing response; progress arrives on the event stream.
When the thread already has a turn running the prompt is queued instead:
`queued` is true and `turn` is 0 (the turn number is assigned when the
prompt is dispatched).

reviewer_ids.description: Omitted by older clients to preserve the current/default selection.

reviewer_overrides.description: Omitted by older clients to preserve existing reviewer overrides.

UpdateSessionRequest.description: Partial session update (rename / archive). Omitted fields are unchanged.

UpdateThreadRequest.description:
Partial thread update between turns (mode/model switching). Rejected with
a conflict while a turn is running. Omitted fields are unchanged.

model_options.description: Replaces the thread's model options when present.

permission_mode.description:
Permission policy for each fresh automation session. Omitted by older
clients means Ask.

workspace_id.description: Required for workspace scope: whose `.agents/.mcp.json` to edit.

UpsertModeRequest.description:
Create or update a user-level mode (`<config>/modes/<id>.toml`). Saving
under a built-in id customizes that built-in.

oneOf.description: None uses the global default permission mode.

default_thinking_level.description: None uses the global default thinking level.

UpsertProviderRequest.description:
Create or update a provider. The API key (when given) goes to the secret
store, never to the config file.

UpsertReviewerProfileRequest.description:
Create or update a custom reviewer profile. Omit `id` to create one;
built-in profiles cannot be changed through this request.

cached_input_tokens.description: Cached/read tokens where the provider reports them.

context_window.description:
The model's context window as reported live by the provider during
the turn. Authoritative over any static catalog value.

cost_usd.description: Estimated cost in USD, when list pricing for the model is known.

UsageSummary.description: Aggregated usage for a thread or session.
